countryGate.message: Select a single country (top bar) before importing. Every import is scoped to one country - mixing countries is not allowed.

upload.dropzoneChoose: Choose one or more Excel / CSV files
upload.queuedFiles: {count} more file(s) queued - offered after this import finishes.
upload.headerDetected: Header row detected at line {line} · {count} columns

mapping.profileApplied: Recognised format - mapping profile "{name}" applied automatically{agg}. Review below and adjust if needed.
mapping.aggregateNote: (line items will be combined per record)
mapping.reviewHint: Review the suggested mapping. Unknown columns are kept (never dropped).
mapping.savePrompt: Save this mapping as a reusable profile. Name:

validate.tyreAmountHint: Derived from {qty} tyres × unit cost. Quantity and unit cost are stored; the total is computed so all spend rolls up in one place.
validate.tyreAmountWarn: Looks too high vs your file? Your cost column may already include the quantity — go Back and map it to "Total Amount" instead; the per-tyre price is derived automatically.
validate.granularityTitle: This file looks like line-item / finer-grained data - it may be the wrong module.
validate.granularityBody: The natural key for {module} is {keyLabel}, but {pct}% of keyed rows ({collapsed} of {keyed}) collapse to existing keys. Committing here would discard the line-item detail. If this is per-line data (e.g. parts consumption), import it under a finer-grained module instead. Review before committing.
validate.countryConflictTitle: {count} row(s) carry a country that differs from the selected import country {country}.
validate.countryConflictBody: To protect country isolation these will be committed under {country} only if you explicitly confirm the override. The original country value is preserved in the source row either way.
validate.countryConflictConfirm: I confirm these rows belong to {country} and approve the override.
validate.countryConflictError: {count} row(s) have a country that differs from {country}. Confirm the override to continue.
validate.enrichTitle: {count} row(s) match records that already exist.
validate.enrichCheckbox: Enrich existing records — fill their blank fields from this file (don't skip).
validate.enrichBody: Combines this file with data already on record: it only fills fields that are currently empty and never overwrites a value you already have. Great for stitching together assets/tyres/work orders from different source files. Every change is audited.
validate.forceTitle: {count} row(s) failed validation and will be skipped by default.
validate.forceCheckbox: Force-include these {count} flagged row(s) — commit them anyway.
validate.forceBody: Use this to push through rows you know are acceptable despite a validation warning. Rows that are genuinely un-insertable (e.g. a missing required field) still fail safely at commit and are logged — the rest of the batch is unaffected.

commit.attachBody: Upload a .zip of photos, police reports, invoices, quotations, or insurance docs. Files are stored privately and matched to staged accident rows by claim no, police report no, or asset no. Unmatched files are kept for later reconciliation.
commit.readyWillCommit: {ready} ready + {warning} warning rows will be committed to the live {module} table. Error rows are skipped.
commit.tyreTotalToRecord: Total tyre amount to be recorded: {amount} across {qty} tyres.
commit.roleWarning: Your role can stage but not approve - this will be submitted for approval.
commit.enriching: Enriching existing records… {enriched} enriched · {noMatch} no match
commit.failedResult: No rows could be committed - {failed} row(s) failed. The reasons are listed below.
commit.moreFailed: ...and {count} more - every failed row's reason is saved on the row (Validation issues, code COMMIT_FAILED).
commit.fixAndReimport: Fix the source values (or the column mapping) and re-import the file - committed rows are skipped automatically.
commit.automationNote: Automation: {alerts} tyre-risk alert(s) and {actions} corrective action(s) generated{skipped}.

recent.title: Recent imports
recent.footer: Original files are stored privately; every source row is preserved. Commits run server-side (permission + country scope + idempotency).
recent.reverseCommittedConfirm: Reverse the committed {module} import? This removes the {rows} rows it added to the live {module} table.
recent.deleteStagedConfirm: Delete the {module} import ({status}, {rows} rows)? This permanently removes the staged batch.

orphans.title: Uploaded but not yet imported
orphans.body: These files were uploaded but never completed the wizard, so they added nothing to your live data. Re-run the import from step 1, or remove them.
orphans.deletePrompt: Delete the uploaded file "{name}"? It was never imported, so nothing in your live data is affected.

errors.couldNotProcessAttachment: Could not process the attachment package.

profiles.empty: No saved mappings yet. Upload a file, map its columns, and choose "Save as profile" — it will appear here for reuse.
profiles.deleteConfirm: Delete the saved mapping "{name}" and its {count} column rule(s)? This cannot be undone.
profiles.noRules: No column rules stored for this mapping.

dataLink.intro: Every module links to a vehicle by Asset No. Fleet has {count} vehicle(s). An unlinked row references an asset that doesn't exist in the fleet yet.
dataLink.missingAssets: {count} asset(s) referenced by your data don't exist in Fleet Master yet.
dataLink.repairButton: Create the missing vehicles from tyre data
dataLink.askAdmin: Ask an administrator to run the linkage repair.
dataLink.repairNote: Creates one skeleton vehicle per missing Asset No (country & site copied from its latest tyre record), marked "Auto-created" — complete the details later in Fleet Master. Fully audited.
dataLink.repairConfirm:
Create {count} missing vehicle record(s) from your tyre data?

Each orphan Asset No becomes a skeleton vehicle (country + site copied from its tyres) so every tyre/work-order/inspection links to a real vehicle. You can complete the details later in Fleet Master.
dataLink.repairSuccess: Created {count} vehicle record(s). All linked records now resolve.

costControl.actualAvgSpendNote: from {count} month(s) of real tyre history
costControl.flatRateWarning: Cost check: {count} record(s) ({pct}%) share the SAME cost of {amount}.
costControl.flatRateBody: If your file's cost column already contains the total for the quantity (ERP exports usually do), convert it to per-tyre prices so nothing is double-counted. If the value is just a placeholder, clear it instead.
costControl.convertButton: My costs are line totals — convert to per-tyre
costControl.clearButton: It's a placeholder — clear these costs
costControl.applyActualsButton: Set every vehicle's budget from its actual spend
costControl.applyActualsNote: Uses each vehicle's own real tyre history (total spend ÷ its active months). Overrides are audited and feed Budget Planner, forecasting, and the executive digest.
costControl.adminOnly: Budget overrides are admin-only. Ask an administrator to set budgets.
costControl.errorLoad: Could not load the budget overview.
costControl.errorInvalidAmount: Enter a valid amount (0 or more).
costControl.successOverride: Monthly budget set to {amount} on {count} vehicle(s).
costControl.successConvert: Converted {count} record(s) to per-tyre prices — total spend is now {amount} (matches your file).
costControl.successClear: Cleared the flat {amount} placeholder from {count} record(s). Costs are now honest — upload a file with real costs to fill them.
costControl.successApply: Applied actual-spend budgets to {count} vehicle(s) — total {amount} / month.
costControl.convertConfirm:
Your file's cost column already contains the TOTAL for the quantity (ERP style)?

This converts existing records to true per-tyre prices (cost ÷ qty) so every page and report shows your file's real totals — no double counting. Audited. Run once per import style.
costControl.clearConfirm:
{count} record(s) carry the SAME cost of {amount} ({pct}% of all costed tyres).

A single identical value across all records is a default/placeholder, not real market cost — it makes every cost KPI wrong.

Clear these costs? (They become blank — pages show 0/— honestly. Real costs return when you upload a file with a cost column, using "Enrich existing records".)
costControl.applyActualsConfirm:
Set EVERY vehicle's monthly tyre budget to its own actual average monthly spend (computed from its real tyre history)?

This overwrites current budgets and is fully audited.

completeness.footer: Fields under 40% leave their analytics pages empty. Fill them by uploading a file that contains the column with Enrich existing records on — values merge into the same rows by Serial / Asset No, never overwriting existing data.
